title: Removable Media Mount Events on Engineering Workstations

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for CIP-010-4 R6 Part 6.1.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NERC CIP CIP-010-4 R6 Part 6.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.13.54: Removable Media Mount Events on Engineering Workstations.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.13.54' running on index=wineventlog EventCode 6416 or Sysmon EventCode 12/1, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=6416 earliest=-7d
| lookup bes_engineering_hosts.csv ComputerName OUTPUT bes_system
| where isnotnull(bes_system)
| stats count by ComputerName, DeviceName, ClassName, bes_system
| sort - count
description: We detect USB or removable media usage on BES engineering workstations to enforce transient media controls.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes wineventlog and sourcetypes WinEventLog:Security. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Enable device installation auditing. (2) Optionally deploy Sysmon for richer context. (3) Weekly review with OT engineering. (4) Document approved maintenance media in lookup. Indexes required: `index=wineventlog`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security`.
visualization: Table (mounts), Bar chart (by host), Single value (distinct devices). Key fields: `ClassName`, `ComputerName`, `DeviceName`, `bes_system`.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Patches deferred for compatibility testing, vendor lag on stable releases, or planned outage windows.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=wineventlog` `EventCode` 6416 or Sysmon EventCode 12/1.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Enable device installation auditing. (2) Optionally deploy Sysmon for richer context. (3) Weekly review with OT engineering. (4) Document approved maintenance media in lookup.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=6416 earliest=-7d
| lookup bes_engineering_hosts.csv ComputerName OUTPUT bes_system
| where isnotnull(bes_system)
| stats count by ComputerName, DeviceName, ClassName, bes_system
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Removable Media Mount Events on Engineering Workstations (CIP-010-4 R6 Part 6.1)** — We detect USB or removable media usage on BES engineering workstations to enforce transient media controls.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=wineventlog` `EventCode` 6416 or Sysmon EventCode 12/1. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: wineventlog; **sourcetype**: WinEventLog:Security. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=wineventlog, sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(bes_system)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by ComputerName, DeviceName, ClassName, bes_system** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Removable Media Mount Events on Engineering Workstations (CIP-010-4 R6 Part 6.1)** — We detect USB or removable media usage on BES engineering workstations to enforce transient media controls.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=wineventlog` `EventCode` 6416 or Sysmon EventCode 12/1. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Endpoint.Processes` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ESP firewall log, Windows Security, or the asset register—whichever the search is built to mirror. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (mounts), Bar chart (by host), Single value (distinct devices)
grandmaExplanation: We detect USB or removable media usage on BES engineering workstations to enforce transient media controls. That is important for the big power grid, where proof needs to line up with what the rules expect.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for the Authentication data model (Authentication.Authentication).
